message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Mathematical infectious disease models available in literature, mostly take in their design that the parameters of basic reproduction number
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    and interval serial
                    <jats:italic>S</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>
                      <jats:italic>I</jats:italic>
                    </jats:sub>
                    as constant values during tracking the outbreak cases. In this report a new intelligent model called HH-COVID-19 is proposed, with simple design and adaptive parameters.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    The parameters
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    and
                    <jats:italic>S</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>
                      <jats:italic>I</jats:italic>
                    </jats:sub>
                    are adapted by adding three new weighting factors
                    <jats:italic>α, β</jats:italic>
                    and
                    <jats:italic>γ</jats:italic>
                    and two free parameters
                    <jats:italic>σ</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>1</jats:sub>
                    and
                    <jats:italic>σ</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>2</jats:sub>
                    in function of time
                    <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                    , thus the HH-COVID-19 become time-variant model. The parameters
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    ,
                    <jats:italic>S</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>
                      <jats:italic>I</jats:italic>
                    </jats:sub>
                    ,
                    <jats:italic>α, β, γ, σ</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>1</jats:sub>
                    and
                    <jats:italic>σ</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>2</jats:sub>
                    are estimated optimally based on a recent algorithm of artificial intelligence (AI), inspired from nature called Harris Hawks Optimizer (HHO), using the data of the confirmed infected cases in Algeria country in the first
                    <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                    = 55 days.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Parameters estimated optimally:
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    = 1.341,
                    <jats:italic>S</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>
                      <jats:italic>I</jats:italic>
                    </jats:sub>
                    = 5.991,
                    <jats:italic>α</jats:italic>
                    = 2.987,
                    <jats:italic>β</jats:italic>
                    = 1.566,
                    <jats:italic>γ</jats:italic>
                    = 4.998,
                    <jats:italic>σ</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>1</jats:sub>
                    =
                    <jats:italic>−</jats:italic>
                    0.133 and
                    <jats:italic>σ</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>2</jats:sub>
                    = 0.0324.
                    <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    starts on 1.341 and ends to 2.677, and
                    <jats:italic>S</jats:italic>
                    <jats:sub>
                      <jats:italic>I</jats:italic>
                    </jats:sub>
                    starts on 5.991 and ends to 6.692. The estimated results are identically to the actual infected incidence in Algeria, HH-COVID-19 proved its superiority in comparison study. HH-COVID-19 predicts that in 1 May, the infected cases exceed 50 000, during May, to reach quickly the herd immunity stage at beginning of July.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>HH-COVID-19 can be used for tracking any COVID-19 outbreak cases around the world, just should updating its new parameters to fitting the area to be studied, especially when the population is directly vulnerable to COVID-19 infection.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: A new design of an adaptive model of infectious diseases based on artificial intelligence approach: monitoring and forecasting of COVID-19 epidemic cases

reference.unstructured: Hamidouche M. COVID-19 outbreak in Algeria: A mathematical Model to predict cumulative cases. Bull World Health Organ. E-pub: 25 March 2020. doi:http://dx.doi.org/10.2471/BLT.20.256065

reference.article-title: Harris hawks optimization: Algorithm and applications
